_note: lint-changed-files hook is PostToolUse (matcher: Write|Edit), best-effort/informational — never blocks. Retargeted to the .mjs port in Phase 2 iteration 2.5. Each case's throwaway case_dir (used as CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR) is guaranteed fresh, so it never contains pyproject.toml/.ruff.toml/an eslintrc/go.mod/Cargo.toml regardless of host tooling — the .py/.ts(no-config)/.go/.rs no-op cases below are therefore deterministic in any environment, not just this one. LINTER-AVAILABILITY FINDING (2.5, re-checked, not inherited from 0.3): ruff/eslint/go/cargo/shellcheck/pyright are all still confirmed absent via `command -v` in this authoring sandbox, AND absent in CI (.github/workflows/ci.yml's reusable job only installs node; only publish.yml's release-dispatch job apt-get-installs shellcheck — ci.yml's actual PR/push gate, the one this fixture corpus really needs to hold under, does NOT, though GitHub's ubuntu-latest hosted runner image typically ships shellcheck preinstalled regardless). npx (bundled with npm, which ships with the official Node distributions, actions/setup-node, and nvm — NOT guaranteed on split-packaged hosts like Debian's nodejs-without-npm apt package, where this one case would fail) is present everywhere this repo's CI and dev environments actually run Node — used below for a genuine, non-fabricated positive emit-path case (F-13, from 0.3's review): the eslint branch's `command -v` gate checks 'npx', not 'eslint' (bash: `run_if_present npx --no-install eslint --no-color "$PATH_INPUT"`), and no eslint package is installed anywhere in this repo's tree (checked: no root .eslintrc*/eslint.config.*, no eslint in package.json) or on any CI runner image by default — so `npx --no-install eslint ...` deterministically fails to resolve eslint and surfaces an `npm error npx canceled ...` diagnostic on stderr, EVERY time, in every environment this repo's CI or dev sandboxes actually run in. This is the real bash-original code path being exercised for real, not a stand-in. FAKE-LINTER-VIA-PATH MECHANISM CONSIDERED AND DECLINED for the .sh/shellcheck case below: tests/hooks/run.sh's per-case "env" field (the only case-level PATH-influencing mechanism the runner exposes) passes each `key=value` straight through to the `env` command via a `jq -r` read with NO shell variable expansion — a value like "$case_dir/bin:$PATH" is passed as that literal 20-character string, not interpolated, and case_dir is a `mktemp -d` path unknown until runtime, so it can't be baked into the static JSON either. There is no way, with the runner's CURRENT field semantics, to prepend a fake-binary directory onto PATH while still preserving whatever PATH entry the host actually needs to resolve `node` itself (verified this would break: this authoring sandbox's `node` lives under an nvm path, not any hardcodable /usr/bin-style default) — so a case authored to rely on that mechanism would not actually run, on this host or any other. Not fabricated; instead the .sh gap (previously zero coverage of any kind, per 0.3's deferral note) is filled with 0.3's other explicitly pre-approved option: a shellcheck-CLEAN sample script, staged as a deterministic no-op that holds identically whether shellcheck is real (clean input -> empty stdout, exit 0) or absent (`command -v` gate no-ops) — verified against the actual hook, both branches converge on `expect_no_context`. SHELLCHECK-CLEAN CLAIM SCOPE (2.5 review): neither the authoring sandbox nor the review environment had a shellcheck binary, so sample.sh's cleanliness is verified by inspection only (trivially clean POSIX: quoted echo, valid shebang, no expansions) — on CI hosts that ship shellcheck (GitHub ubuntu-latest does) the case runs it for real and the no-op determinism rests on that inspection; if shellcheck ever flags it, fix the SAMPLE, not the assertion.

cases.name: typescript-eslint-config-present-delegate-emits-context
cases._note: Positive emit-path case (F-13, mandatory per this iteration): a real .eslintrc.json config gates the eslint branch open; the branch's command-presence gate checks 'npx' (bundled with Node, always present here), not 'eslint' (never installed in this repo). `npx --no-install eslint ...` therefore always fails to resolve eslint and surfaces a real npm/npx diagnostic on stderr — a genuine subprocess run through the real capture/wrap/emit path, not a stand-in. sample.ts is also deliberately invalid syntax so that IF a real eslint were ever reachable in some other environment, a parse error would still guarantee non-empty lint output (deterministic either way, not just deterministic in THIS sandbox's specific absence of eslint).
cases.expect_context: Lint output for the file just changed:

cases.name: shell-shellcheck-clean-noop
cases._note: Deferred .sh/shellcheck case (from 0.3's review follow-up). The shellcheck branch has no config-file gate (command-presence only), so unlike the other extensions a 'clean case_dir' alone doesn't guarantee determinism — it depends on whether shellcheck itself is reachable, which differs between this sandbox (absent) and some CI hosts (may be preinstalled). Staged sample.sh is deliberately shellcheck-clean (correct shebang, no unquoted expansions, no deprecated constructs) so this case holds identically under EITHER a real, clean-passing shellcheck (exit 0, empty stdout -> no context) OR no shellcheck at all (command-presence gate no-ops -> no context) — see the file-level _note for why a fake-binary-via-PATH mechanism was considered and declined instead of this approach.
